refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating multiple requirements and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing elevation and surface features
Create a semantic map identifying and classifying key site features relevant to operations
Integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules
Provide an interface for updating and validating the worksite model based on sensor inputs and operational feedback
selected_dp.selected_dp: Create a semantic map identifying and classifying key site features relevant to operations

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and feature data into a unified model may introduce coupling if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a semantic map DP focuses on logical classification of site features, which can be modularized separately from raw terrain data generation, supporting independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of sufficient data to classify site features accurately
Risk that terrain representation and feature classification may be tightly coupled if not carefully modularized
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the semantic map format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain representation DP from semantic feature classification DP to ensure modularity and reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing and data management functions, including semantic mapping and classification of site features relevant to operations.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and positional data from the Vehicle to build the semantic map.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends operational commands or updates based on the semantic map to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with external systems and remote operators.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as semantic mapping is a core data processing function; minimal diffusion as most data inputs and outputs are internal or through defined interfaces.
Low containment since Vehicle focuses on physical actuation and sensing hardware; semantic mapping would require extensive data flow outside this module.
Moderate containment as teleoperation may use semantic maps for remote control, but mapping itself is not a teleoperation function.
Low containment; safety services may consume semantic map data but do not perform mapping or classification.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the semantic mapping DP because it centralizes data processing and classification functions, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from Vehicle module.
Interface complexity with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules requiring consistent semantic map data formats.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution with too many responsibilities if other data processing tasks are added without clear boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Create a semantic map identifying and classifying key site features relevant to operations' directly satisfies the FR of providing worksite area analysis including terrain representation. The DP clearly addresses the functional requirement by focusing on semantic mapping of key site features, which aligns with the acceptance criteria of generating a worksite model/map for planning and execution.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from Vehicle module may cause diffusion if data interfaces are not well managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single implementation module. The semantic mapping and classification functions are centralized, minimizing diffusion risks. External interfaces are clearly defined, supporting modularity and reducing risk of scattered responsibilities.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives telemetry data, processes it to create the semantic map, and outputs operational commands or updates. The acceptance criteria can be verified by checking the availability and correctness of the generated worksite model/map to planning and execution functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. Ownership is explicitly assigned, supporting traceability and accountability.

recommended_revisions: Establish strict interface contracts and data format standards between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to mitigate diffusion risks and interface complexity.
Monitor and manage the scope of the NGHE Solution module to avoid overloading it with additional responsibilities beyond semantic mapping and classification.
Implement validation and synchronization mechanisms to ensure timely and accurate telemetry data delivery from the Vehicle module to the NGHE Solution module.

decision.justification: The current FR and DP are well aligned and the DP is well contained within a single implementation module with manageable diffusion risks. However, given the high safety criticality and the complexity of semantic mapping and classification, further decomposition of the FR into next-level sub-FRs is warranted to ensure detailed coverage of all critical aspects such as data acquisition, feature classification, terrain representation, and map updating. This will support more precise verification, risk mitigation, and modular design refinement.
next_level_sub_frs: Acquire and validate telemetry data from Vehicle module
Identify and classify key site features relevant to operations
Represent terrain and environmental conditions accurately
Update and maintain the semantic map in real-time

revise.why: To address the identified diffusion risks and interface complexity, the implementation module boundaries and interface contracts should be refined and strictly enforced to ensure modularity and reduce risk of data inconsistency or delays.

assumptions: Telemetry data from Vehicle module is available and reliable with proper interface contracts.
NGHE Solution module has sufficient capacity to handle semantic mapping and classification functions.
Verification methods for semantic map correctness are feasible and defined.
open_risks: Potential delays or inaccuracies in telemetry data affecting map accuracy.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution module if scope expands beyond current functions.
Interface complexity between NGHE Solution and other modules may cause diffusion if not managed.
evidence_needed: Interface contract definitions and data format standards between modules.
Performance metrics of telemetry data delivery and processing latency.
Verification test results of semantic map accuracy and update timeliness.
